requirementAnnotations.reason: States an authenticated user can export their own data as a downloadable CSV file, covering req:export-csv; provenance explicit matches the fixture's expected strength.

Well-formedness (header row, correct delimiter/quoting/escaping, rows matching the user's data) is intrinsic to producing a valid 'CSV file' as the brief states. Not an expected requirement, but a grounded inference; provenance is correctly marked inferred. Same standard applied to the qa-engine escaping/well-formedness elaboration.

Asserts an access-control model (a user may only export data they are authorized to access; unauthenticated requests are rejected). The one-line brief states nothing about authentication or authorization. Behavior is asserted with no basis in the brief or evidence. Same standard applied to the qa-engine unauthenticated-export case.

caseAnnotations.reason: Requests the export and asserts a 200 with Content-Type text/csv, covering the scenario hint (response is a CSV attachment).

Empty-dataset boundary of the stated export capability (header-only CSV when the user has no records). Not an expected scenario, but a reasonable elaboration; provenance is correctly marked inferred. Same standard applied to the qa-engine empty-state case.

Asserts a specific access-control behavior (anonymous request rejected with 401/403, no body returned). The one-line brief states nothing about authentication or an access model. Behavior is asserted with no basis in the brief or evidence. Same standard applied to the qa-engine unauthenticated-export case.

assertionAnnotations.reason: A 200 status on a successful export is directly supported by the stated export capability.
A CSV download declaring a text/csv content type is a reasonable inference from the stated CSV export; provenance is correctly marked inferred.
Zero data rows on an empty dataset is a sound inference; provenance is correctly marked inferred.

The cited evidence (the export-capability statement) does not support a 401 unauthenticated-rejection; no authentication or access model appears in the brief, so the citation does not support the claim. Same standard applied to the qa-engine anonymous-export assertions.
